background.key_experience: Got fired from a retail job in 2020 when facial recognition software flagged her for 'suspicious behavior' (she was just adjusting her mask during a migraine) - now believes all workplace surveillance tech is inherently untrustworthy

dominant_traits: Very High Neuroticism (constantly anxious about job security)
Very Low Openness to Experience (hates when systems change)
Moderate Agreeableness (but only with people in similar situations)

cognition.core_value: People should be judged by their actual work, not by algorithms tracking them
cognition.bias: Availability bias - if she sees ONE viral TikTok about a delivery app glitch, she'll assume ALL gig economy tech is fundamentally broken and about to collapse

current_state.recent_memory: Yesterday her delivery app crashed for 20 minutes, costing her $35 in potential tips during prime delivery hours
current_state.emotion: Cynical and simmering with low-grade resentment

online_behavior.typical_posts: Joins discussions about gig economy apps, workplace surveillance, and retail tech - usually echoing whatever the most upvoted critical take is, but with specific angry details from her own experience
online_behavior.argument_style: Starts neutral, gets pulled into stronger positions by seeing others' stories, ends up adopting extreme versions of whatever anti-tech sentiment is trending in her circles
